It's an old luthier's trick as well.

author: @Blandy929
text: Toilet roll and super glue. If you can get it to stay on the surface you are gluing without it sticking to everything else, it is solid. Goes off with strong vapours and gives off heat.

author: @IJW1874
text: For a greater bond add graphite granules (a pencil lead sanded) to the super glue and bicarb

author: @madmancrow
text:
Funny story ive been using this for years great for plastic and rebuilding broken items

I never new how strong it was till my dad broke his ute mirror coming from a 4 hr drive,

End at old mate place we end up using baling twine and tone super glue and layers of bicarb.  Its now been 3 year and the rest of ute falling apart

Tho that mirror still holding strong

author: @changingmyworld-j9z
text: If it's a wind turbine to go outside I would have thought MDF is a bad choice of material.

author: @roboneal
text:
I've used coffee creamer, sawdust and cinnamon before as well... at work... or at home when out of baking soda. I've actually used drops of glue and baking soda to rebuild tabs on old electronic cases. It's not as flexible as the original plastics but works in a pinch and can be filed. You can use wool to fill bad holes in as well if you have a stripped hole.  I wonder if the reaction is simply a surface area catalyst or an another reaction...I will have to read a bit up on this.

Thank you! I have been enjoying these videos.

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank You Robert!!
This is how I have managed to glue plastic to wood, and it really works well!!

author: @Mrnichols92
text: You could use any fine particulate. Sand is a good option

replies.author: @philip5940
replies.text: Bicarbonate of soda produces decent heat which may contribute to a good bond . Sand is inert but a good aggregate material . Probably can't be filed into shape though .

author: @MrPanchito62
text: I used that on some of our products and got laughed at, till they saw that it worked magnificently.

author: @scottbdivers724
text: Looks like Anthony Hopkins lost brother. How about Super Glue Hardener. They sell it. Xylene in a spray bottle. ??? Insta set.brand name.

author: @MrPanchoak
text:
Just about any fine powder works well for that.
Fine sawdust, ash, what have you at hand.

author: @immanutt4442
text: wayment ... silence of the lambs

author: @dualmode1
text: Try water, it also works with superclue

author: @salilsahani2721
text: Thank you :)

author: @jonnyBravos
text: Mitre bond.. all day super glue with a hardening agent in a spray,, just don't breath in the stuff

author: @modelracecar
text:
Superglue and bicarb sets as hard as concrete.
You might want to try superglue and cotton wool as a filler or superglue and toilet paper to reinforcing or fix broken thin parts.

author: @jimplantinga6288
text:
I heard that this method is strong enough for repairs to propellers, on helicopters. 
it definitely works well on fiberglass boats...

author: @seymourclearly
text: So many useful tips on this channel

author: @craftymulligar
text: You can also use to repair fingernails with good file and sandpaper. Shape and look like a fingernail, but don't last long. No real cure for me.

author: @eby6114
text: I believe this has been strength tested and the accelerated glue has less power than slow dry.  It's still strong however.

author: @Arcticpagan
text: This method is used to fix chips in propellers on turbo prop planes.

author: @RobeiroVideos
text: I see a video with súper glue and  graphite from a pencil very more strong, but I do not test Yet.

author: @moriartythemundane
text: Instead of baking soda, get some lead out of a pencil, preferably a 2b or higher. Scrape the lead into a powder and add to the glue as before, that works even stronger!!

author: @Therapistinthewhitehouse
text: I’m not sure MDF is a good substrate to use outside.

author: @Alkimi
text: Would love to know more about the reaction that takes place. This has been fascinating me lately

author: @JoeNathan42420
text: I've heard that it makes it harder but in my experience it actually makes it weaker

replies.author: @philip5940
replies.text: Harder doesn't mean stronger and weaker doesn't mean softer .

author: @Flashahol
text: I have rebuilt plastic case corners using this trick!

author: @kevinleebailey
text: Super glue (isocyanoacrylate?) and sodium bicarbonate 3D printing anyone ?

author: @src938
text: I used to use this method and it works well, however I now use Mitre Bond, a lot less messy

author: @AutoNomades
text: Would be interesting to see a test with fast cement powder

author: @jaesallstrom5705
text: and old guitar fretnut repairing trick...

author: @ianbottom7396
text: It’s a great hack but works well provided there isn’t too much excess superglue otherwise it’s hard on top, still wet and unreacted underneath

author: @rayg436
text: I recently fixed my toilet flapper with this trick 👍

author: @angelusmendez5084
text: Great tip, I didn't know that 👏

author: @billl605
text: some videos suggest powdered graphite works better than bicarbonate of soda

author: @ScottAuch
text: Personally, I go the hot glue route. 😊

replies.author: @philip5940
replies.text: For a good bond on plastic, heat may be the ticket.

author: @corringhamdepot4434
text: Apparently graphite powder makes a much harder bond, according to a video I saw recently.

replies.author: @ScottAuch
replies.text: You beat me to the comment

replies.author: @philip5940
replies.text: It makes a harder cast. The bond strength is a separate property yet to be assessed which I'm curious about.

author: @gregcooper8407
text: With the speed you make, edit and upload videos it’s a miracle you ever have time to make anything!  I only left the comment that these were a pain to make yesterday lol

author: @BrownBabyJesus
text: I used this technique several years ago to repair a pair of headphones I dropped gently onto my desk - it took 6 months to break the original construction and my repair (augmented with string reinforcement) lasted 2 years before replacement and is no doubt still very intact in a landfill somewhere 😂😂

author: @RichardCranium321
text:
a friend of mine is a helicopter mechanic, he said they use this exact method to repair small chips in the propeller. they'd build it up with this and then sand it back smooth. He keeps a small pill bottle of sodium bicarbonate and a few of the "single use" krazyglue super glue sticks in another bottle, but he always has them in his little "go bag" toolbox...

one morning on his way to work he was pulled over & the officer noticed the open toolbox with an unmarked pill bottle full of a strange white powdery substance... 

he said it was a strange conversation at first, but after he explained everything and showed the officer how it worked, he let the officer do his roadside test and then was allowed to leave, but he said his sphincter was puckered for the entire traffic stop! 😆👍🏽

author: @Vile_Entity_3545
text: Or cotton wool, sawdust and sand.

author: @richbuilds_com
text: Great for filling gaps too. You can even "cast" it in a mould for small stuff.

replies.author: @philip5940
replies.text: Powdered ground charcoal was demonstrated to be much harder and stronger than using bicarbonate of soda. Bicarbonate may however give a better bond on account it produces a hot reaction which may produce better adhesion to plastic.

author: @Buzzhumma
text: I might see if I can fix some borer holes in my didgeridoo with this method 👍🏻

author: @Buzzhumma
text: Ah yes . I remember seeing this to rebuild a fret board bridge on a guitar repair video . Great ! 👍🏻

author: @Julkkis1980
text: Epoxy and normal flour works very well in gluing and as a filler as well. Built boats with it in stitch n glue.

replies.author: @alakazaam-n7c
replies.text: This video explains to add baking soda to super glue, so the same method with cooking dough flour and epoxy. I am also working on building a boat out a fiber glass

text: I'll have to remember that if I try making this kinda thing again

author: @brett4264
text: Good to know. Thanks.

author: @MarinusMakesStuff
text: Rock hard, but brittle as fhook. I would go the contact cement way, but if you really want a good bond, use polymax. Polymax is definitely the best glue in this situation.

replies.author: @brett4264
replies.text: Are you talking about "UHU Polymax"? That's what a web search came up with in a search for Polymax glue.

author: @susangordon1157
text: Wonderfully helpful tip! Thank you!
